abawd_enforced_2026_detail: ABAWD time limit is actively enforced statewide in FY2026. Georgia implemented the OBBBA/HR1 (2025) ABAWD changes effective Nov. 1, 2025, applied to applications and recertifications submitted on/after that date. The current 36-month time-limit period is Dec. 1, 2023 through Nov. 30, 2026. All Georgia counties are time-limited ABAWD counties; no geographic waiver is in effect.
qualifying_org_definition: A community service program site serving a useful public purpose in health, social service, environmental protection, education, urban/rural development, welfare, recreation, public facilities, public safety, or child care. Comparable Workfare is an unsalaried placement at a supervised worksite with a public or private nonprofit provider (state, federal, county, and city government agencies may also sponsor). The ABAWD may self-initiate at any such site; no MOU, pre-approved provider list, or county registration of the site is required.
qualifyingOrgShort: Any nonprofit/for-profit/public community-service site serving a useful public purpose, self-arranged by the ABAWD; no state registration or approved-provider list required.
verification_instrument: Form 805 'ABAWD Volunteer Work Requirement Verification Form' (also referred to as the 'Comparable Workfare Activity Form'; available in English and Spanish). PART I is completed by the SNAP case manager to assign the number of required work-activity hours (the allotment-over-minimum-wage figure); PART II is completed by the local organization/provider staff after completion of the volunteer hours, attesting the named person participated satisfactorily and completed N hours in month/year. The ABAWD self-arranges placement, has the provider complete the form, and submits it to their SNAP eligibility specialist/case manager.
verificationFormName: Form 805 (ABAWD Volunteer Work Requirement Verification Form / Comparable Workfare Activity Form)
reportingCadence: At application and at recertification (renewal). Comparable workfare participation must be verified at application and recertification; no monthly reporting is required. Verification is tied to certification periods, not a monthly submission cycle.
requiredPrestep: none (no enrollment, IEP/EDP, orientation, or provider registration required to use the comparable-workfare/volunteer lane). NUANCE: the SNAP case manager calculates the required monthly hours via the allotment-over-minimum-wage formula and records that figure in Form 805 PART I; this is a county-side computation/documentation step, not a recipient-facing gate or placement assignment. The ABAWD self-arranges placement and begins hours without pre-approval. (Distinct from the separate SNAP Works E&T track, which DOES require an Individual Employment Plan/orientation; ABAWDs are not required to enroll in SNAP Works to use comparable workfare.)
accepts_outofstate_detail: Georgia policy (PAMMS 3355) and Form 805 impose no geographic restriction on the comparable-workfare provider. No language requires the organization to be Georgia-based or registered in Georgia; the ABAWD is responsible for 'arranging their own placement at a nonprofit or for-profit provider' with no stated in-state requirement. Form 805 requests only organization name, address, phone, and supervisor info (no EIN, no Georgia charitable-registration field, no notarization). Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24) any qualifying provider may serve absent a state restriction, so an out-of-state 501(c)(3) completing Form 805 should operationally be accepted, but the state is formally silent.
hours_formula_detail: Georgia's comparable-workfare/volunteer lane does NOT use the flat federal 80-hour default. PAMMS 3355 states: 'The number of monthly required hours is obtained by dividing the SNAP AU's monthly allotment by the federal minimum wage. For example: $292 (allotment amount)/$7.25 (current minimum wage) = 40.27; the ABAWD must complete 40 hours per month for comparable workfare.' Use whole numbers and do not round (truncate). Hours may not exceed 30 hours weekly. Thus required monthly hours = floor(monthly SNAP allotment / $7.25). For a one-person household at the FY2026 max allotment (~$298 in the 48 contiguous states), this is floor(298/7.25) = 41 hours; the policy's worked example ($292) yields 40. Lower allotments yield fewer hours. NOTE: the FLAT 80 hrs/month (20 hrs/week) figure in DHS press materials applies to paid work and approved E&T-program participation, NOT to the comparable-workfare volunteer lane, which uses the allotment/minimum-wage formula.
formulaDetail: Required monthly hours = floor(monthly SNAP allotment / $7.25 federal minimum wage), truncated to a whole number, capped at 30 hours/week (~130/month). Policy example: $292/$7.25 = 40.27 -> 40 hours. At FY2026 one-person max allotment (~$298): floor(298/7.25) = 41 hours. Georgia uses the $7.25 FEDERAL minimum wage in the formula (the nominal Georgia state minimum wage is $5.15 but does not apply to FLSA-covered work; PAMMS 3355 explicitly cites $7.25).
minimum_wage_detail: PAMMS 3355 uses the federal minimum wage of $7.25/hr in the comparable-workfare formula and explicitly labels it '($7.25 (current minimum wage))'. Georgia's statutory state minimum wage is $5.15/hr but applies only to employers not covered by the FLSA; virtually all employers are FLSA-covered, so $7.25 governs. No sub-state/regional minimum wages exist in Georgia. The higher-of-state-vs-federal rule yields $7.25.
geographic_waivers: none in FY2026 — ABAWD enforced statewide. Georgia held no statewide ABAWD time-limit waiver prior to OBBBA and has no county/area geographic waivers in effect for FY2026; all counties are time-limited ABAWD counties. OBBBA (HR1, 2025) eliminated the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment standard waiver authority (remaining authority limited to areas above 10% unemployment or noncontiguous AK/HI). Georgia is not on the USDA FNS list of states with FY2025 waiver responses and has no FY2026 waiver on file.
geographicWaivers: None in FY2026. Georgia has no statewide or county/area ABAWD time-limit waiver in effect; all counties are time-limited and fully enforced. OBBBA (2025) removed the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment waiver authority nationwide. No Georgia FY2026 waiver is on file with USDA FNS.
submissionMechanism: Paper Form 805. The comparable-workfare provider completes PART II (and the case manager completes PART I with the required-hours figure); the completed form is submitted to the recipient's SNAP eligibility specialist/case manager at the county DFCS office for case documentation. (Georgia also accepts SNAP documents via the Georgia Gateway portal/ConnectFS document upload and county fax/mail in practice, but PAMMS 3355 specifies the form is provided to the case manager; channel beyond 'to the case manager' is not specified in 3355 itself.)
submission_deadline: At application and at recertification (renewal); no specific within-month deadline is stated. PAMMS notes participation must be verified timely to prevent over-issuance of benefits, but no fixed monthly reporting deadline is set. Verification is tied to certification periods.
submissionDeadline: At application and at recertification; no fixed monthly deadline. Must be verified timely to avoid over-issuance, but no specific within-month date is stated in policy.
notes:
FY2026 VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Re-confirmed Georgia classification = workfare_formula against primary sources retrieved THIS pass.

(1) PAMMS 3355 (authoritative DFCS SNAP policy manual, fetched directly) confirmed VERBATIM the comparable-workfare formula: 'The number of monthly required hours is obtained by dividing the SNAP AU's monthly allotment by the federal minimum wage. For example: $292 (allotment amount)/$7.25 (current minimum wage) = 40.27; the ABAWD must complete 40 hours per month for comparable workfare.' 'Hours may not exceed 30 hours weekly.' Use whole numbers, do not round. This formula is DISTINCT from the flat 80 hrs/month that applies to paid work and approved E&T-program participation. The ABAWD 'arranging their own placement at a nonprofit or for-profit provider' — self-initiated, no pre-approval list.

(2) DFCS public ABAWD page confirmed the general self-initiation lane VERBATIM: 'ABAWDs may self-initiate a Comparable Workfare activity at community service program sites that are serving a useful community purpose' across the standard 7 CFR 273.7 useful-purpose fields. Comparable Workfare described as 'an unsalaried placement at a supervised worksite with a public or private non-profit provider.' Form 805 (English + Spanish) is the verification document.

(3) Form 805 PDF (dfcs.georgia.gov) was downloaded and its embedded text extracted locally. Confirmed title 'State of Georgia ABAWD VOLUNTEER WORK VERIFICATION FORM' / footer 'Form 805 (01/19) ABAWD Comparable Workfare.' Structure: PART I 'To be completed by case manager to assign number of work activity hours required' (fields: case manager name/phone/fax, Work Activity Type = Comparable Workfare, Required hours per month, Participation Month, Client Name, Client ID #, Case #); PART II 'To be completed by local organization staff after completion of work activity hours' (Organization Name, Organization Address, Organization Phone#, Volunteer Supervisor Name; attestation 'The person named above is participating in a satisfactory manner Yes/No and completed ___ hours in the month of __/__ (month/year)'; Printed Name of Volunteer Supervisor; Signature of Volunteer Supervisor/Date). No EIN, no recipient DOB/address, no notarization, no in-state requirement, no approved-provider-list box.

(4) ENFORCEMENT: DHS press release (Nov 1, 2025) confirms OBBBA/HR1 ABAWD implementation effective Nov. 1, 2025, applied to applications/recerts on/after that date; 36-month period Dec. 1, 2023 - Nov. 30, 2026; standard work figure stated as '20 hours a week or 80 hours a month' (this is the paid-work/E&T-program figure, NOT the comparable-workfare formula).

(5) MINIMUM WAGE: PAMMS 3355 explicitly uses $7.25 federal minimum wage in the formula. Georgia's nominal state minimum is $5.15/hr but does not apply to FLSA-covered work (virtually all employers), so $7.25 governs and is the figure the policy itself cites. No sub-state/regional wages.

(6) WAIVERS: Georgia has no statewide or area ABAWD time-limit waiver in FY2026 (all counties time-limited). OBBBA eliminated the insufficient-jobs/high-unemployment waiver authority nationwide. The FNS FY2025-2029 waiver page could not be fetched directly this pass (repeated 60s timeouts), but multiple WebSearch syntheses and the absence of Georgia from FY2025 waiver-response state lists corroborate no Georgia waiver; this is the one direct-fetch gap (see unresolved).

(7) COUNTY VARIATION: false. Georgia SNAP is STATE-administered (PAMMS is a single statewide manual; Form 805 is statewide). County DFCS offices apply the same policy; no material county-level variation in the volunteer lane.

CONFIDENCE: high — classification (workfare_formula), the exact allotment/$7.25 formula, the $7.25 wage, self-initiation with no prestep, and Form 805 are all directly confirmed by the governing PAMMS 3355 manual text, the DFCS public page, AND the extracted Form 805 PDF. The only field relying on search synthesis rather than a single quoted primary document is the FY2026 waiver status (FNS page timed out), but it is well-corroborated and consistent with prior passes.

PRIOR-PASS NOTES (retained from research/abawd-operability/states/GA.json): adversarial review across PAMMS 3355/3360/3350 found no hidden prestep, MOU, EDP, or approved-site registry; PAMMS 3360 confirmed the IEP requirement applies only to SNAP Works E&T enrollees, not to ABAWDs using comparable workfare; PAMMS 3350 work registration is a standard universal eligibility step, not an ABAWD-volunteer gate.
conflictsWithPrior: monthly_hours_required: prior file 3355/states record used 40 (the policy's $292 worked example). Verified record uses 41 to reflect monthlyHoursAtMaxAllotment (floor(298/7.25) at the FY2026 one-person max allotment). Same formula, different representative anchor — not a substantive policy change; actual required hours vary by each AU's allotment.

required_prestep: prior file said 'none' with confidence; verified record keeps 'none' for the recipient but adds the nuance that the case manager computes/records the required-hours figure in Form 805 PART I (a county-side computation, not a recipient gate or placement assignment). No change to the conclusion that the recipient needs no enrollment/EDP/registration.
remote_stance: prior file 'unknown' -> normalized to 'silent' (no Georgia source addresses remote/virtual volunteering, but none prohibits it).
county_variation_flag: confirmed false (state-administered); unchanged. Added normalized 'countyVariation': false.

Added normalized superset fields (classification, enforced2026, qualifyingOrgShort, verificationFormName, requiredPrestep, remoteStance, outOfStateNonprofit, minimumWageUsd, regionalWages, monthlyHoursAtMaxAllotment, formulaDetail, geographicWaivers, submissionMechanism, submissionDeadline, reportingCadence, countyVariation, primary_source_urls) without altering prior substantive findings.
unresolved: FNS FY2025-2029 ABAWD waiver page (https://www.fna.usda.gov/snap/abawd/waivers/2025-2029) could not be fetched directly this pass (repeated 60s timeouts); Georgia 'no FY2026 waiver' is corroborated via WebSearch synthesis and the absence of Georgia from FY2025 waiver-response state lists, not a single quoted FNS line. Conclusion is consistent and high-confidence but not directly quoted from the FNS page this pass.
Remote/virtual volunteering eligibility is not addressed in any Georgia primary source (PAMMS 3355, DFCS page, Form 805) — treated as 'silent', not affirmatively allowed or prohibited.

Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is not affirmatively stated; inferred 'silent/likely-allowed' because PAMMS 3355 and Form 805 impose no in-state or registration requirement, but no source explicitly confirms cross-state providers are accepted.

Submission channel beyond 'provide the completed Form 805 to the SNAP case manager/eligibility specialist' is not specified in PAMMS 3355; Georgia Gateway/ConnectFS document upload, fax, and mail are used in general SNAP practice but are not named in 3355 for Form 805 specifically.

The 'ABAWD Comparable Workfare Guide' PDF and the FFY26 E&T State Plan PDF were not machine-readable via fetch in prior passes (403 / binary-compressed); the clean reading rests on the directly-fetched PAMMS 3355 manual text, the DFCS public page, and the extracted Form 805 PDF.
